'''Yahoko Shrine''' (やほこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548}}, {{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}.

=== History ===
It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}. In {{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 7), it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} of Junior Fifth Upper. In {{ill|lt=1163|Q19672|WD=Q19672}} ({{ill|lt=Chōkan|Q1193904|WD=Q1193904}} 1), it became an {{ill|lt=imperial-prayer temple/shrine|Q22131160|WD=Q22131160}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nijō|Q440149|WD=Q440149}}, and a dark-blue-with-gold ''{{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212}}'' (later a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}<ref name="読売20220501"/>) was dedicated, along with donation of five {{ill|lt=tan (land unit)|Q11410513|WD=Q11410513|lt=tan}} of land as {{ill|lt=temple/shrine estate|Q11458058|WD=Q11458058|lt=sacred estate}}. According to ''{{ill|lt=Tosa Diary|Q844452|WD=Q844452}}'', {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|Q468288|WD=Q468288}} prayed there to avoid {{ill|lt=pirates|Q45393|WD=Q45393}}.

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} enshrining {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}}, related to the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}''. Its standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and standing male deity statue are designated national Important Cultural Properties. Among them, the shrine-owned paper document ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1163, Important Cultural Property<ref name="読売20220501">"Treasure of the region preserved for posterity / repository completed in Tokushima" {{ill|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|Q645218|WD=Q645218}}, morning edition, May 1, 2022 (Tsumugu page)</ref>) is said to be the oldest surviving document in Tokushima Prefecture. On March 27, 2022, a completion ceremony was held for the storage building preserving these cultural properties<ref name="読売20220501"/>.

In {{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 3), the shrine received an imperial visit by {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito}}. The bettō temple {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yahoko-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} is also associated.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
*:plus 34 other deities
*Regarding the deity, ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' and ''Awa-shi'' identify it as '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}'''; ''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'' and ''Dai Nihonshi Jingi-shi'' state that Yahoko deity is son or great-grandson of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>Nobukazu Nobukawa, "Suwa clan genealogy," ''Revised Suwa Genealogy, Main Volume'' (Iida Kōtarō, {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 10), p.18</ref><ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年">Hoga Toshio, ''Descendants of Nagasunehiko and Their Worshiped Shrines'', 2008</ref>. In the "Naga clan genealogy" in volume 6 of ''Shokeifu'', '''Yagami-no-Mikoto''' appears as child of Iroto-no-Mikoto (“Iroto” meaning younger sibling), and this is considered to correspond to Yahoko deity, namely {{ill|lt=Yaki-no-Mikoto|Q65249081|WD=Q65249081}}<ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年"/>.

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okuyama Shrine
* Tenjin Shrine
* Yama Shrine
* Taishōgun Shrine

=== Cultural properties ===
; Important Cultural Properties (national)
* Wooden standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, height 108.8 cm
* Wooden standing male deity statue, height 81.2 cm
* Paper manuscript ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1 scroll) (with 8-scroll dark-blue-gold Lotus Sutra)

=== Access ===
* About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Anan Station|Q4751223|WD=Q4751223}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478}} ({{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}).

=== Notes and references ===

* ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603)

'''Yahoko Shrine''' (やほこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548}}, {{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}.

=== History ===
It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}. In {{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 7), it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} of Junior Fifth Upper. In {{ill|lt=1163|Q19672|WD=Q19672}} ({{ill|lt=Chōkan|Q1193904|WD=Q1193904}} 1), it became an {{ill|lt=imperial-prayer temple/shrine|Q22131160|WD=Q22131160}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nijō|Q440149|WD=Q440149}}, and a dark-blue-with-gold ''{{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212}}'' (later a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}<ref name="読売20220501"/>) was dedicated, along with donation of five {{ill|lt=tan (land unit)|Q11410513|WD=Q11410513|lt=tan}} of land as {{ill|lt=temple/shrine estate|Q11458058|WD=Q11458058|lt=sacred estate}}. According to ''{{ill|lt=Tosa Diary|Q844452|WD=Q844452}}'', {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|Q468288|WD=Q468288}} prayed there to avoid {{ill|lt=pirates|Q45393|WD=Q45393}}.

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} enshrining {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}}, related to the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}''. Its standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and standing male deity statue are designated national Important Cultural Properties. Among them, the shrine-owned paper document ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1163, Important Cultural Property<ref name="読売20220501">"Treasure of the region preserved for posterity / repository completed in Tokushima" {{ill|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|Q645218|WD=Q645218}}, morning edition, May 1, 2022 (Tsumugu page)</ref>) is said to be the oldest surviving document in Tokushima Prefecture. On March 27, 2022, a completion ceremony was held for the storage building preserving these cultural properties<ref name="読売20220501"/>.

In {{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 3), the shrine received an imperial visit by {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito}}. The bettō temple {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yahoko-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} is also associated.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}}
*:plus 34 other deities
*Regarding the deity, ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' and ''Awa-shi'' identify it as '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}'''; ''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'' and ''Dai Nihonshi Jingi-shi'' state that Yahoko deity is son or great-grandson of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>Nobukazu Nobukawa, "Suwa clan genealogy," ''Revised Suwa Genealogy, Main Volume'' (Iida Kōtarō, {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 10), p.18</ref><ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年">Hoga Toshio, ''Descendants of Nagasunehiko and Their Worshiped Shrines'', 2008</ref>. In the "Naga clan genealogy" in volume 6 of ''Shokeifu'', '''Yagami-no-Mikoto''' appears as child of Iroto-no-Mikoto (“Iroto” meaning younger sibling), and this is considered to correspond to Yahoko deity, namely {{ill|lt=Yaki-no-Mikoto|Q65249081|WD=Q65249081}}<ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年"/>.

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okuyama Shrine
* Tenjin Shrine
* Yama Shrine
* Taishōgun Shrine

=== Cultural properties ===
; Important Cultural Properties (national)
* Wooden standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, height 108.8 cm
* Wooden standing male deity statue, height 81.2 cm
* Paper manuscript ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1 scroll) (with 8-scroll dark-blue-gold Lotus Sutra)

=== Access ===
* About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Anan Station|Q4751223|WD=Q4751223}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478}} ({{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}).

=== Notes and references ===

{{nihongo|'''Yahoko Shrine'''|八桙神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社

|創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}
|本殿=

'''Yahoko Shrine''' (やほこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548}}, {{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}.

=== History ===
It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}. In {{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 7), it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} of Junior Fifth Upper. In {{ill|lt=1163|Q19672|WD=Q19672}} ({{ill|lt=Chōkan|Q1193904|WD=Q1193904}} 1), it became an {{ill|lt=imperial-prayer temple/shrine|Q22131160|WD=Q22131160}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nijō|Q440149|WD=Q440149}}, and a dark-blue-with-gold ''{{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212}}'' (later a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}<ref name="読売20220501"/>) was dedicated, along with donation of five {{ill|lt=tan (land unit)|Q11410513|WD=Q11410513|lt=tan}} of land as {{ill|lt=temple/shrine estate|Q11458058|WD=Q11458058|lt=sacred estate}}. According to ''{{ill|lt=Tosa Diary|Q844452|WD=Q844452}}'', {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|Q468288|WD=Q468288}} prayed there to avoid {{ill|lt=pirates|Q45393|WD=Q45393}}.

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} enshrining {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}}, related to the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}''. Its standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and standing male deity statue are designated national Important Cultural Properties. Among them, the shrine-owned paper document ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1163, Important Cultural Property<ref name="読売20220501">"Treasure of the region preserved for posterity / repository completed in Tokushima" {{ill|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|Q645218|WD=Q645218}}, morning edition, May 1, 2022 (Tsumugu page)</ref>) is said to be the oldest surviving document in Tokushima Prefecture. On March 27, 2022, a completion ceremony was held for the storage building preserving these cultural properties<ref name="読売20220501"/>.

In {{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 3), the shrine received an imperial visit by {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito}}. The bettō temple {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yahoko-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} is also associated.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}}
*:plus 34 other deities
*Regarding the deity, ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' and ''Awa-shi'' identify it as '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}'''; ''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'' and ''Dai Nihonshi Jingi-shi'' state that Yahoko deity is son or great-grandson of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>Nobukazu Nobukawa, "Suwa clan genealogy," ''Revised Suwa Genealogy, Main Volume'' (Iida Kōtarō, {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 10), p.18</ref><ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年">Hoga Toshio, ''Descendants of Nagasunehiko and Their Worshiped Shrines'', 2008</ref>. In the "Naga clan genealogy" in volume 6 of ''Shokeifu'', '''Yagami-no-Mikoto''' appears as child of Iroto-no-Mikoto (“Iroto” meaning younger sibling), and this is considered to correspond to Yahoko deity, namely {{ill|lt=Yaki-no-Mikoto|Q65249081|WD=Q65249081}}<ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年"/>.

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okuyama Shrine
* Tenjin Shrine
* Yama Shrine
* Taishōgun Shrine

=== Cultural properties ===
; Important Cultural Properties (national)
* Wooden standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, height 108.8 cm
* Wooden standing male deity statue, height 81.2 cm
* Paper manuscript ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1 scroll) (with 8-scroll dark-blue-gold Lotus Sutra)

=== Access ===
* About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Anan Station|Q4751223|WD=Q4751223}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478}} ({{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}).

=== Notes and references ===
{{Reflist}}
* ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603)

{{nihongo|'''Yahoko Shrine'''|八桙神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{神社

|創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}
|本殿=

'''Yahoko Shrine''' (やほこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548}}, {{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}.

=== History ===
It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}. In {{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 7), it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} of Junior Fifth Upper. In {{ill|lt=1163|Q19672|WD=Q19672}} ({{ill|lt=Chōkan|Q1193904|WD=Q1193904}} 1), it became an {{ill|lt=imperial-prayer temple/shrine|Q22131160|WD=Q22131160}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nijō|Q440149|WD=Q440149}}, and a dark-blue-with-gold ''{{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212}}'' (later a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}<ref name="読売20220501"/>) was dedicated, along with donation of five {{ill|lt=tan (land unit)|Q11410513|WD=Q11410513|lt=tan}} of land as {{ill|lt=temple/shrine estate|Q11458058|WD=Q11458058|lt=sacred estate}}. According to ''{{ill|lt=Tosa Diary|Q844452|WD=Q844452}}'', {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|Q468288|WD=Q468288}} prayed there to avoid {{ill|lt=pirates|Q45393|WD=Q45393}}.

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} enshrining {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}}, related to the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}''. Its standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and standing male deity statue are designated national Important Cultural Properties. Among them, the shrine-owned paper document ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1163, Important Cultural Property<ref name="読売20220501">"Treasure of the region preserved for posterity / repository completed in Tokushima" {{ill|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|Q645218|WD=Q645218}}, morning edition, May 1, 2022 (Tsumugu page)</ref>) is said to be the oldest surviving document in Tokushima Prefecture. On March 27, 2022, a completion ceremony was held for the storage building preserving these cultural properties<ref name="読売20220501"/>.

In {{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 3), the shrine received an imperial visit by {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito}}. The bettō temple {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yahoko-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} is also associated.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}}
*:plus 34 other deities
*Regarding the deity, ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' and ''Awa-shi'' identify it as '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}'''; ''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'' and ''Dai Nihonshi Jingi-shi'' state that Yahoko deity is son or great-grandson of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>Nobukazu Nobukawa, "Suwa clan genealogy," ''Revised Suwa Genealogy, Main Volume'' (Iida Kōtarō, {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 10), p.18</ref><ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年">Hoga Toshio, ''Descendants of Nagasunehiko and Their Worshiped Shrines'', 2008</ref>. In the "Naga clan genealogy" in volume 6 of ''Shokeifu'', '''Yagami-no-Mikoto''' appears as child of Iroto-no-Mikoto (“Iroto” meaning younger sibling), and this is considered to correspond to Yahoko deity, namely {{ill|lt=Yaki-no-Mikoto|Q65249081|WD=Q65249081}}<ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年"/>.

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okuyama Shrine
* Tenjin Shrine
* Yama Shrine
* Taishōgun Shrine

=== Cultural properties ===
; Important Cultural Properties (national)
* Wooden standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, height 108.8 cm
* Wooden standing male deity statue, height 81.2 cm
* Paper manuscript ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1 scroll) (with 8-scroll dark-blue-gold Lotus Sutra)

=== Access ===
* About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Anan Station|Q4751223|WD=Q4751223}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478}} ({{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}).

=== Notes and references ===
{{Reflist}}
* ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603)

{{nihongo|'''Yahoko Shrine'''|八桙神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称= Yahoko Shrine
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=阿南市}}
|所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}{{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583}}{{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548}} Miyauchi 463

|祭神= {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}}
|社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}
|創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}
|本殿= 
|別名= Yahoko Shrine
|例祭=

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''Yahoko Shrine''' (やほこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548}}, {{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}.

=== History ===
It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}. In {{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 7), it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} of Junior Fifth Upper. In {{ill|lt=1163|Q19672|WD=Q19672}} ({{ill|lt=Chōkan|Q1193904|WD=Q1193904}} 1), it became an {{ill|lt=imperial-prayer temple/shrine|Q22131160|WD=Q22131160}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nijō|Q440149|WD=Q440149}}, and a dark-blue-with-gold ''{{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212}}'' (later a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}<ref name="読売20220501"/>) was dedicated, along with donation of five {{ill|lt=tan (land unit)|Q11410513|WD=Q11410513|lt=tan}} of land as {{ill|lt=temple/shrine estate|Q11458058|WD=Q11458058|lt=sacred estate}}. According to ''{{ill|lt=Tosa Diary|Q844452|WD=Q844452}}'', {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|Q468288|WD=Q468288}} prayed there to avoid {{ill|lt=pirates|Q45393|WD=Q45393}}.

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} enshrining {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944}}, related to the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031}}''. Its standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and standing male deity statue are designated national Important Cultural Properties. Among them, the shrine-owned paper document ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1163, Important Cultural Property<ref name="読売20220501">"Treasure of the region preserved for posterity / repository completed in Tokushima" {{ill|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|Q645218|WD=Q645218}}, morning edition, May 1, 2022 (Tsumugu page)</ref>) is said to be the oldest surviving document in Tokushima Prefecture. On March 27, 2022, a completion ceremony was held for the storage building preserving these cultural properties<ref name="読売20220501"/>.

In {{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 3), the shrine received an imperial visit by {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito}}. The bettō temple {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yahoko-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} is also associated.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}}
*:plus 34 other deities
*Regarding the deity, ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' and ''Awa-shi'' identify it as '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}'''; ''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'' and ''Dai Nihonshi Jingi-shi'' state that Yahoko deity is son or great-grandson of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}<ref>Nobukazu Nobukawa, "Suwa clan genealogy," ''Revised Suwa Genealogy, Main Volume'' (Iida Kōtarō, {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 10), p.18</ref><ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年">Hoga Toshio, ''Descendants of Nagasunehiko and Their Worshiped Shrines'', 2008</ref>. In the "Naga clan genealogy" in volume 6 of ''Shokeifu'', '''Yagami-no-Mikoto''' appears as child of Iroto-no-Mikoto (“Iroto” meaning younger sibling), and this is considered to correspond to Yahoko deity, namely {{ill|lt=Yaki-no-Mikoto|Q65249081|WD=Q65249081}}<ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年"/>.

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okuyama Shrine
* Tenjin Shrine
* Yama Shrine
* Taishōgun Shrine

=== Cultural properties ===
; Important Cultural Properties (national)
* Wooden standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, height 108.8 cm
* Wooden standing male deity statue, height 81.2 cm
* Paper manuscript ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1 scroll) (with 8-scroll dark-blue-gold Lotus Sutra)

=== Access ===
* About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Anan Station|Q4751223|WD=Q4751223}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478}} ({{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}).

=== Notes and references ===
{{Reflist}}
* ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603)

=== External links ===
* [http://www.city.anan.tokushima.jp/docs/2010120700030/ Yahoko Shrine, Anan City]

{{nihongo|'''Yahoko Shrine'''|八桙神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

'''Yahoko Shrine''' (やほこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548|1=Nagao-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642|1=753}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}. In {{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7), it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Junior Fifth Upper. In {{ill|lt=1163|Q19672|WD=Q19672|1=1163}} ({{ill|lt=Chōkan|Q1193904|WD=Q1193904|1=Chōkan}} 1), it became an {{ill|lt=imperial-prayer temple/shrine|Q22131160|WD=Q22131160|1=Chokugansho}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nijō|Q440149|WD=Q440149|1=Emperor Nijō}}, and a dark-blue-with-gold ''{{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212|1=Lotus Sutra}}'' (later a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}<ref name="読売20220501"/>) was dedicated, along with donation of five {{ill|lt=tan (land unit)|Q11410513|WD=Q11410513|lt=tan|1=tan}} of land as {{ill|lt=temple/shrine estate|Q11458058|WD=Q11458058|lt=sacred estate|1=Jisha-ryō}}. According to ''{{ill|lt=Tosa Diary|Q844452|WD=Q844452|1=Tosa Nikki}}'', {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|Q468288|WD=Q468288|1=Ki no Tsurayuki}} prayed there to avoid {{ill|lt=pirates|Q45393|WD=Q45393|1=Piracy}}.

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} enshrining {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, related to the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}''. Its standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and standing male deity statue are designated national Important Cultural Properties. Among them, the shrine-owned paper document ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1163, Important Cultural Property<ref name="読売20220501">"Treasure of the region preserved for posterity / repository completed in Tokushima" {{ill|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|Q645218|WD=Q645218|1=The Yomiuri Shimbun}}, morning edition, May 1, 2022 (Tsumugu page)</ref>) is said to be the oldest surviving document in Tokushima Prefecture. On March 27, 2022, a completion ceremony was held for the storage building preserving these cultural properties<ref name="読売20220501"/>.

In {{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3), the shrine received an imperial visit by {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito|1=Naruhito}}. The bettō temple {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yahoko-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} is also associated.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
*:plus 34 other deities
*Regarding the deity, ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' and ''Awa-shi'' identify it as '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}'''; ''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'' and ''Dai Nihonshi Jingi-shi'' state that Yahoko deity is son or great-grandson of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}<ref>Nobukazu Nobukawa, "Suwa clan genealogy," ''Revised Suwa Genealogy, Main Volume'' (Iida Kōtarō, {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 10), p.18</ref><ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年">Hoga Toshio, ''Descendants of Nagasunehiko and Their Worshiped Shrines'', 2008</ref>. In the "Naga clan genealogy" in volume 6 of ''Shokeifu'', '''Yagami-no-Mikoto''' appears as child of Iroto-no-Mikoto (“Iroto” meaning younger sibling), and this is considered to correspond to Yahoko deity, namely {{ill|lt=Yaki-no-Mikoto|Q65249081|WD=Q65249081|1=Yachinomi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年"/>.

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okuyama Shrine
* Tenjin Shrine
* Yama Shrine
* Taishōgun Shrine

=== Cultural properties ===
; Important Cultural Properties (national)
* Wooden standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, height 108.8 cm
* Wooden standing male deity statue, height 81.2 cm
* Paper manuscript ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1 scroll) (with 8-scroll dark-blue-gold Lotus Sutra)

=== Access ===
* About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Anan Station|Q4751223|WD=Q4751223|1=Anan Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} ({{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}).

=== Notes and references ===

{{nihongo|'''Yahoko Shrine'''|八桙神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

'''Yahoko Shrine''' (やほこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548|1=Nagao-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642|1=753}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}. In {{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7), it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Junior Fifth Upper. In {{ill|lt=1163|Q19672|WD=Q19672|1=1163}} ({{ill|lt=Chōkan|Q1193904|WD=Q1193904|1=Chōkan}} 1), it became an {{ill|lt=imperial-prayer temple/shrine|Q22131160|WD=Q22131160|1=Chokugansho}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nijō|Q440149|WD=Q440149|1=Emperor Nijō}}, and a dark-blue-with-gold ''{{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212|1=Lotus Sutra}}'' (later a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}<ref name="読売20220501"/>) was dedicated, along with donation of five {{ill|lt=tan (land unit)|Q11410513|WD=Q11410513|lt=tan|1=tan}} of land as {{ill|lt=temple/shrine estate|Q11458058|WD=Q11458058|lt=sacred estate|1=Jisha-ryō}}. According to ''{{ill|lt=Tosa Diary|Q844452|WD=Q844452|1=Tosa Nikki}}'', {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|Q468288|WD=Q468288|1=Ki no Tsurayuki}} prayed there to avoid {{ill|lt=pirates|Q45393|WD=Q45393|1=Piracy}}.

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} enshrining {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, related to the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}''. Its standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and standing male deity statue are designated national Important Cultural Properties. Among them, the shrine-owned paper document ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1163, Important Cultural Property<ref name="読売20220501">"Treasure of the region preserved for posterity / repository completed in Tokushima" {{ill|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|Q645218|WD=Q645218|1=The Yomiuri Shimbun}}, morning edition, May 1, 2022 (Tsumugu page)</ref>) is said to be the oldest surviving document in Tokushima Prefecture. On March 27, 2022, a completion ceremony was held for the storage building preserving these cultural properties<ref name="読売20220501"/>.

In {{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3), the shrine received an imperial visit by {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito|1=Naruhito}}. The bettō temple {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yahoko-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} is also associated.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
*:plus 34 other deities
*Regarding the deity, ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' and ''Awa-shi'' identify it as '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}'''; ''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'' and ''Dai Nihonshi Jingi-shi'' state that Yahoko deity is son or great-grandson of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}<ref>Nobukazu Nobukawa, "Suwa clan genealogy," ''Revised Suwa Genealogy, Main Volume'' (Iida Kōtarō, {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 10), p.18</ref><ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年">Hoga Toshio, ''Descendants of Nagasunehiko and Their Worshiped Shrines'', 2008</ref>. In the "Naga clan genealogy" in volume 6 of ''Shokeifu'', '''Yagami-no-Mikoto''' appears as child of Iroto-no-Mikoto (“Iroto” meaning younger sibling), and this is considered to correspond to Yahoko deity, namely {{ill|lt=Yaki-no-Mikoto|Q65249081|WD=Q65249081|1=Yachinomi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年"/>.

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okuyama Shrine
* Tenjin Shrine
* Yama Shrine
* Taishōgun Shrine

=== Cultural properties ===
; Important Cultural Properties (national)
* Wooden standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, height 108.8 cm
* Wooden standing male deity statue, height 81.2 cm
* Paper manuscript ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1 scroll) (with 8-scroll dark-blue-gold Lotus Sutra)

=== Access ===
* About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Anan Station|Q4751223|WD=Q4751223|1=Anan Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} ({{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}).

=== Notes and references ===

'''Yahoko Shrine''' (やほこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548|1=Nagao-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642|1=753}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}. In {{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7), it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Junior Fifth Upper. In {{ill|lt=1163|Q19672|WD=Q19672|1=1163}} ({{ill|lt=Chōkan|Q1193904|WD=Q1193904|1=Chōkan}} 1), it became an {{ill|lt=imperial-prayer temple/shrine|Q22131160|WD=Q22131160|1=Chokugansho}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nijō|Q440149|WD=Q440149|1=Emperor Nijō}}, and a dark-blue-with-gold ''{{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212|1=Lotus Sutra}}'' (later a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}<ref name="読売20220501"/>) was dedicated, along with donation of five {{ill|lt=tan (land unit)|Q11410513|WD=Q11410513|lt=tan|1=tan}} of land as {{ill|lt=temple/shrine estate|Q11458058|WD=Q11458058|lt=sacred estate|1=Jisha-ryō}}. According to ''{{ill|lt=Tosa Diary|Q844452|WD=Q844452|1=Tosa Nikki}}'', {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|Q468288|WD=Q468288|1=Ki no Tsurayuki}} prayed there to avoid {{ill|lt=pirates|Q45393|WD=Q45393|1=Piracy}}.

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} enshrining {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, related to the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}''. Its standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and standing male deity statue are designated national Important Cultural Properties. Among them, the shrine-owned paper document ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1163, Important Cultural Property<ref name="読売20220501">"Treasure of the region preserved for posterity / repository completed in Tokushima" {{ill|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|Q645218|WD=Q645218|1=The Yomiuri Shimbun}}, morning edition, May 1, 2022 (Tsumugu page)</ref>) is said to be the oldest surviving document in Tokushima Prefecture. On March 27, 2022, a completion ceremony was held for the storage building preserving these cultural properties<ref name="読売20220501"/>.

In {{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3), the shrine received an imperial visit by {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito|1=Naruhito}}. The bettō temple {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yahoko-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} is also associated.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
*:plus 34 other deities
*Regarding the deity, ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' and ''Awa-shi'' identify it as '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}'''; ''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'' and ''Dai Nihonshi Jingi-shi'' state that Yahoko deity is son or great-grandson of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}<ref>Nobukazu Nobukawa, "Suwa clan genealogy," ''Revised Suwa Genealogy, Main Volume'' (Iida Kōtarō, {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 10), p.18</ref><ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年">Hoga Toshio, ''Descendants of Nagasunehiko and Their Worshiped Shrines'', 2008</ref>. In the "Naga clan genealogy" in volume 6 of ''Shokeifu'', '''Yagami-no-Mikoto''' appears as child of Iroto-no-Mikoto (“Iroto” meaning younger sibling), and this is considered to correspond to Yahoko deity, namely {{ill|lt=Yaki-no-Mikoto|Q65249081|WD=Q65249081|1=Yachinomi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年"/>.

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okuyama Shrine
* Tenjin Shrine
* Yama Shrine
* Taishōgun Shrine

=== Cultural properties ===
; Important Cultural Properties (national)
* Wooden standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, height 108.8 cm
* Wooden standing male deity statue, height 81.2 cm
* Paper manuscript ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1 scroll) (with 8-scroll dark-blue-gold Lotus Sutra)

=== Access ===
* About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Anan Station|Q4751223|WD=Q4751223|1=Anan Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} ({{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}).

=== Notes and references ===

{{nihongo|'''Yahoko Shrine'''|八桙神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

|創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642|1=753}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}

'''Yahoko Shrine''' (やほこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548|1=Nagao-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642|1=753}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}. In {{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7), it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Junior Fifth Upper. In {{ill|lt=1163|Q19672|WD=Q19672|1=1163}} ({{ill|lt=Chōkan|Q1193904|WD=Q1193904|1=Chōkan}} 1), it became an {{ill|lt=imperial-prayer temple/shrine|Q22131160|WD=Q22131160|1=Chokugansho}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nijō|Q440149|WD=Q440149|1=Emperor Nijō}}, and a dark-blue-with-gold ''{{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212|1=Lotus Sutra}}'' (later a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}<ref name="読売20220501"/>) was dedicated, along with donation of five {{ill|lt=tan (land unit)|Q11410513|WD=Q11410513|lt=tan|1=tan}} of land as {{ill|lt=temple/shrine estate|Q11458058|WD=Q11458058|lt=sacred estate|1=Jisha-ryō}}. According to ''{{ill|lt=Tosa Diary|Q844452|WD=Q844452|1=Tosa Nikki}}'', {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|Q468288|WD=Q468288|1=Ki no Tsurayuki}} prayed there to avoid {{ill|lt=pirates|Q45393|WD=Q45393|1=Piracy}}.

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} enshrining {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, related to the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}''. Its standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and standing male deity statue are designated national Important Cultural Properties. Among them, the shrine-owned paper document ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1163, Important Cultural Property<ref name="読売20220501">"Treasure of the region preserved for posterity / repository completed in Tokushima" {{ill|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|Q645218|WD=Q645218|1=The Yomiuri Shimbun}}, morning edition, May 1, 2022 (Tsumugu page)</ref>) is said to be the oldest surviving document in Tokushima Prefecture. On March 27, 2022, a completion ceremony was held for the storage building preserving these cultural properties<ref name="読売20220501"/>.

In {{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3), the shrine received an imperial visit by {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito|1=Naruhito}}. The bettō temple {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yahoko-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} is also associated.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
*:plus 34 other deities
*Regarding the deity, ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' and ''Awa-shi'' identify it as '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}'''; ''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'' and ''Dai Nihonshi Jingi-shi'' state that Yahoko deity is son or great-grandson of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}<ref>Nobukazu Nobukawa, "Suwa clan genealogy," ''Revised Suwa Genealogy, Main Volume'' (Iida Kōtarō, {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 10), p.18</ref><ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年">Hoga Toshio, ''Descendants of Nagasunehiko and Their Worshiped Shrines'', 2008</ref>. In the "Naga clan genealogy" in volume 6 of ''Shokeifu'', '''Yagami-no-Mikoto''' appears as child of Iroto-no-Mikoto (“Iroto” meaning younger sibling), and this is considered to correspond to Yahoko deity, namely {{ill|lt=Yaki-no-Mikoto|Q65249081|WD=Q65249081|1=Yachinomi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年"/>.

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okuyama Shrine
* Tenjin Shrine
* Yama Shrine
* Taishōgun Shrine

=== Cultural properties ===
; Important Cultural Properties (national)
* Wooden standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, height 108.8 cm
* Wooden standing male deity statue, height 81.2 cm
* Paper manuscript ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1 scroll) (with 8-scroll dark-blue-gold Lotus Sutra)

=== Access ===
* About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Anan Station|Q4751223|WD=Q4751223|1=Anan Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} ({{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}).

=== Notes and references ===

{{nihongo|'''Yahoko Shrine'''|八桙神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

'''Yahoko Shrine''' (やほこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548|1=Nagao-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642|1=753}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}. In {{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7), it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Junior Fifth Upper. In {{ill|lt=1163|Q19672|WD=Q19672|1=1163}} ({{ill|lt=Chōkan|Q1193904|WD=Q1193904|1=Chōkan}} 1), it became an {{ill|lt=imperial-prayer temple/shrine|Q22131160|WD=Q22131160|1=Chokugansho}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nijō|Q440149|WD=Q440149|1=Emperor Nijō}}, and a dark-blue-with-gold ''{{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212|1=Lotus Sutra}}'' (later a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}<ref name="読売20220501"/>) was dedicated, along with donation of five {{ill|lt=tan (land unit)|Q11410513|WD=Q11410513|lt=tan|1=tan}} of land as {{ill|lt=temple/shrine estate|Q11458058|WD=Q11458058|lt=sacred estate|1=Jisha-ryō}}. According to ''{{ill|lt=Tosa Diary|Q844452|WD=Q844452|1=Tosa Nikki}}'', {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|Q468288|WD=Q468288|1=Ki no Tsurayuki}} prayed there to avoid {{ill|lt=pirates|Q45393|WD=Q45393|1=Piracy}}.

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} enshrining {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, related to the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}''. Its standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and standing male deity statue are designated national Important Cultural Properties. Among them, the shrine-owned paper document ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1163, Important Cultural Property<ref name="読売20220501">"Treasure of the region preserved for posterity / repository completed in Tokushima" {{ill|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|Q645218|WD=Q645218|1=The Yomiuri Shimbun}}, morning edition, May 1, 2022 (Tsumugu page)</ref>) is said to be the oldest surviving document in Tokushima Prefecture. On March 27, 2022, a completion ceremony was held for the storage building preserving these cultural properties<ref name="読売20220501"/>.

In {{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3), the shrine received an imperial visit by {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito|1=Naruhito}}. The bettō temple {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yahoko-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} is also associated.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
*:plus 34 other deities
*Regarding the deity, ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' and ''Awa-shi'' identify it as '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}'''; ''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'' and ''Dai Nihonshi Jingi-shi'' state that Yahoko deity is son or great-grandson of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}<ref>Nobukazu Nobukawa, "Suwa clan genealogy," ''Revised Suwa Genealogy, Main Volume'' (Iida Kōtarō, {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 10), p.18</ref><ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年">Hoga Toshio, ''Descendants of Nagasunehiko and Their Worshiped Shrines'', 2008</ref>. In the "Naga clan genealogy" in volume 6 of ''Shokeifu'', '''Yagami-no-Mikoto''' appears as child of Iroto-no-Mikoto (“Iroto” meaning younger sibling), and this is considered to correspond to Yahoko deity, namely {{ill|lt=Yaki-no-Mikoto|Q65249081|WD=Q65249081|1=Yachinomi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年"/>.

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okuyama Shrine
* Tenjin Shrine
* Yama Shrine
* Taishōgun Shrine

=== Cultural properties ===
; Important Cultural Properties (national)
* Wooden standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, height 108.8 cm
* Wooden standing male deity statue, height 81.2 cm
* Paper manuscript ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1 scroll) (with 8-scroll dark-blue-gold Lotus Sutra)

=== Access ===
* About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Anan Station|Q4751223|WD=Q4751223|1=Anan Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} ({{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}).

'''Yahoko Shrine''' (やほこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548|1=Nagao-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642|1=753}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}. In {{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7), it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Junior Fifth Upper. In {{ill|lt=1163|Q19672|WD=Q19672|1=1163}} ({{ill|lt=Chōkan|Q1193904|WD=Q1193904|1=Chōkan}} 1), it became an {{ill|lt=imperial-prayer temple/shrine|Q22131160|WD=Q22131160|1=Chokugansho}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nijō|Q440149|WD=Q440149|1=Emperor Nijō}}, and a dark-blue-with-gold ''{{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212|1=Lotus Sutra}}'' (later a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}<ref name="読売20220501"/>) was dedicated, along with donation of five {{ill|lt=tan (land unit)|Q11410513|WD=Q11410513|lt=tan|1=tan}} of land as {{ill|lt=temple/shrine estate|Q11458058|WD=Q11458058|lt=sacred estate|1=Jisha-ryō}}. According to ''{{ill|lt=Tosa Diary|Q844452|WD=Q844452|1=Tosa Nikki}}'', {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|Q468288|WD=Q468288|1=Ki no Tsurayuki}} prayed there to avoid {{ill|lt=pirates|Q45393|WD=Q45393|1=Piracy}}.

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} enshrining {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, related to the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}''. Its standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and standing male deity statue are designated national Important Cultural Properties. Among them, the shrine-owned paper document ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1163, Important Cultural Property<ref name="読売20220501">"Treasure of the region preserved for posterity / repository completed in Tokushima" {{ill|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|Q645218|WD=Q645218|1=The Yomiuri Shimbun}}, morning edition, May 1, 2022 (Tsumugu page)</ref>) is said to be the oldest surviving document in Tokushima Prefecture. On March 27, 2022, a completion ceremony was held for the storage building preserving these cultural properties<ref name="読売20220501"/>.

In {{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3), the shrine received an imperial visit by {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito|1=Naruhito}}. The bettō temple {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yahoko-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} is also associated.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
*:plus 34 other deities
*Regarding the deity, ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' and ''Awa-shi'' identify it as '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}'''; ''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'' and ''Dai Nihonshi Jingi-shi'' state that Yahoko deity is son or great-grandson of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}<ref>Nobukazu Nobukawa, "Suwa clan genealogy," ''Revised Suwa Genealogy, Main Volume'' (Iida Kōtarō, {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 10), p.18</ref><ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年">Hoga Toshio, ''Descendants of Nagasunehiko and Their Worshiped Shrines'', 2008</ref>. In the "Naga clan genealogy" in volume 6 of ''Shokeifu'', '''Yagami-no-Mikoto''' appears as child of Iroto-no-Mikoto (“Iroto” meaning younger sibling), and this is considered to correspond to Yahoko deity, namely {{ill|lt=Yaki-no-Mikoto|Q65249081|WD=Q65249081|1=Yachinomi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年"/>.

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okuyama Shrine
* Tenjin Shrine
* Yama Shrine
* Taishōgun Shrine

=== Cultural properties ===
; Important Cultural Properties (national)
* Wooden standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, height 108.8 cm
* Wooden standing male deity statue, height 81.2 cm
* Paper manuscript ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1 scroll) (with 8-scroll dark-blue-gold Lotus Sutra)

=== Access ===
* About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Anan Station|Q4751223|WD=Q4751223|1=Anan Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} ({{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}).

|創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642|1=753}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}

'''Yahoko Shrine''' (やほこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548|1=Nagao-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642|1=753}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}. In {{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7), it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Junior Fifth Upper. In {{ill|lt=1163|Q19672|WD=Q19672|1=1163}} ({{ill|lt=Chōkan|Q1193904|WD=Q1193904|1=Chōkan}} 1), it became an {{ill|lt=imperial-prayer temple/shrine|Q22131160|WD=Q22131160|1=Chokugansho}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nijō|Q440149|WD=Q440149|1=Emperor Nijō}}, and a dark-blue-with-gold ''{{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212|1=Lotus Sutra}}'' (later a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}<ref name="読売20220501"/>) was dedicated, along with donation of five {{ill|lt=tan (land unit)|Q11410513|WD=Q11410513|lt=tan|1=tan}} of land as {{ill|lt=temple/shrine estate|Q11458058|WD=Q11458058|lt=sacred estate|1=Jisha-ryō}}. According to ''{{ill|lt=Tosa Diary|Q844452|WD=Q844452|1=Tosa Nikki}}'', {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|Q468288|WD=Q468288|1=Ki no Tsurayuki}} prayed there to avoid {{ill|lt=pirates|Q45393|WD=Q45393|1=Piracy}}.

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} enshrining {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, related to the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}''. Its standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and standing male deity statue are designated national Important Cultural Properties. Among them, the shrine-owned paper document ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1163, Important Cultural Property<ref name="読売20220501">"Treasure of the region preserved for posterity / repository completed in Tokushima" {{ill|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|Q645218|WD=Q645218|1=The Yomiuri Shimbun}}, morning edition, May 1, 2022 (Tsumugu page)</ref>) is said to be the oldest surviving document in Tokushima Prefecture. On March 27, 2022, a completion ceremony was held for the storage building preserving these cultural properties<ref name="読売20220501"/>.

In {{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3), the shrine received an imperial visit by {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito|1=Naruhito}}. The bettō temple {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yahoko-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} is also associated.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
*:plus 34 other deities
*Regarding the deity, ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' and ''Awa-shi'' identify it as '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}'''; ''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'' and ''Dai Nihonshi Jingi-shi'' state that Yahoko deity is son or great-grandson of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}<ref>Nobukazu Nobukawa, "Suwa clan genealogy," ''Revised Suwa Genealogy, Main Volume'' (Iida Kōtarō, {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 10), p.18</ref><ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年">Hoga Toshio, ''Descendants of Nagasunehiko and Their Worshiped Shrines'', 2008</ref>. In the "Naga clan genealogy" in volume 6 of ''Shokeifu'', '''Yagami-no-Mikoto''' appears as child of Iroto-no-Mikoto (“Iroto” meaning younger sibling), and this is considered to correspond to Yahoko deity, namely {{ill|lt=Yaki-no-Mikoto|Q65249081|WD=Q65249081|1=Yachinomi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年"/>.

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okuyama Shrine
* Tenjin Shrine
* Yama Shrine
* Taishōgun Shrine

=== Cultural properties ===
; Important Cultural Properties (national)
* Wooden standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, height 108.8 cm
* Wooden standing male deity statue, height 81.2 cm
* Paper manuscript ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1 scroll) (with 8-scroll dark-blue-gold Lotus Sutra)

=== Access ===
* About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Anan Station|Q4751223|WD=Q4751223|1=Anan Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} ({{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}).

|創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642|1=753}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}

'''Yahoko Shrine''' (やほこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548|1=Nagao-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642|1=753}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}. In {{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7), it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Junior Fifth Upper. In {{ill|lt=1163|Q19672|WD=Q19672|1=1163}} ({{ill|lt=Chōkan|Q1193904|WD=Q1193904|1=Chōkan}} 1), it became an {{ill|lt=imperial-prayer temple/shrine|Q22131160|WD=Q22131160|1=Chokugansho}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nijō|Q440149|WD=Q440149|1=Emperor Nijō}}, and a dark-blue-with-gold ''{{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212|1=Lotus Sutra}}'' (later a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}<ref name="読売20220501"/>) was dedicated, along with donation of five {{ill|lt=tan (land unit)|Q11410513|WD=Q11410513|lt=tan|1=tan}} of land as {{ill|lt=temple/shrine estate|Q11458058|WD=Q11458058|lt=sacred estate|1=Jisha-ryō}}. According to ''{{ill|lt=Tosa Diary|Q844452|WD=Q844452|1=Tosa Nikki}}'', {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|Q468288|WD=Q468288|1=Ki no Tsurayuki}} prayed there to avoid {{ill|lt=pirates|Q45393|WD=Q45393|1=Piracy}}.

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} enshrining {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, related to the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}''. Its standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and standing male deity statue are designated national Important Cultural Properties. Among them, the shrine-owned paper document ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1163, Important Cultural Property<ref name="読売20220501">"Treasure of the region preserved for posterity / repository completed in Tokushima" {{ill|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|Q645218|WD=Q645218|1=The Yomiuri Shimbun}}, morning edition, May 1, 2022 (Tsumugu page)</ref>) is said to be the oldest surviving document in Tokushima Prefecture. On March 27, 2022, a completion ceremony was held for the storage building preserving these cultural properties<ref name="読売20220501"/>.

In {{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3), the shrine received an imperial visit by {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito|1=Naruhito}}. The bettō temple {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yahoko-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} is also associated.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
*:plus 34 other deities
*Regarding the deity, ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' and ''Awa-shi'' identify it as '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}'''; ''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'' and ''Dai Nihonshi Jingi-shi'' state that Yahoko deity is son or great-grandson of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}<ref>Nobukazu Nobukawa, "Suwa clan genealogy," ''Revised Suwa Genealogy, Main Volume'' (Iida Kōtarō, {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 10), p.18</ref><ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年">Hoga Toshio, ''Descendants of Nagasunehiko and Their Worshiped Shrines'', 2008</ref>. In the "Naga clan genealogy" in volume 6 of ''Shokeifu'', '''Yagami-no-Mikoto''' appears as child of Iroto-no-Mikoto (“Iroto” meaning younger sibling), and this is considered to correspond to Yahoko deity, namely {{ill|lt=Yaki-no-Mikoto|Q65249081|WD=Q65249081|1=Yachinomi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年"/>.

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okuyama Shrine
* Tenjin Shrine
* Yama Shrine
* Taishōgun Shrine

=== Cultural properties ===
; Important Cultural Properties (national)
* Wooden standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, height 108.8 cm
* Wooden standing male deity statue, height 81.2 cm
* Paper manuscript ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1 scroll) (with 8-scroll dark-blue-gold Lotus Sutra)

=== Access ===
* About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Anan Station|Q4751223|WD=Q4751223|1=Anan Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} ({{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}).

|創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642|1=753}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}
|本殿=

'''Yahoko Shrine''' (やほこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548|1=Nagao-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642|1=753}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}. In {{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7), it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Junior Fifth Upper. In {{ill|lt=1163|Q19672|WD=Q19672|1=1163}} ({{ill|lt=Chōkan|Q1193904|WD=Q1193904|1=Chōkan}} 1), it became an {{ill|lt=imperial-prayer temple/shrine|Q22131160|WD=Q22131160|1=Chokugansho}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nijō|Q440149|WD=Q440149|1=Emperor Nijō}}, and a dark-blue-with-gold ''{{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212|1=Lotus Sutra}}'' (later a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}<ref name="読売20220501"/>) was dedicated, along with donation of five {{ill|lt=tan (land unit)|Q11410513|WD=Q11410513|lt=tan|1=tan}} of land as {{ill|lt=temple/shrine estate|Q11458058|WD=Q11458058|lt=sacred estate|1=Jisha-ryō}}. According to ''{{ill|lt=Tosa Diary|Q844452|WD=Q844452|1=Tosa Nikki}}'', {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|Q468288|WD=Q468288|1=Ki no Tsurayuki}} prayed there to avoid {{ill|lt=pirates|Q45393|WD=Q45393|1=Piracy}}.

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} enshrining {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, related to the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}''. Its standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and standing male deity statue are designated national Important Cultural Properties. Among them, the shrine-owned paper document ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1163, Important Cultural Property<ref name="読売20220501">"Treasure of the region preserved for posterity / repository completed in Tokushima" {{ill|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|Q645218|WD=Q645218|1=The Yomiuri Shimbun}}, morning edition, May 1, 2022 (Tsumugu page)</ref>) is said to be the oldest surviving document in Tokushima Prefecture. On March 27, 2022, a completion ceremony was held for the storage building preserving these cultural properties<ref name="読売20220501"/>.

In {{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3), the shrine received an imperial visit by {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito|1=Naruhito}}. The bettō temple {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yahoko-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} is also associated.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
*:plus 34 other deities
*Regarding the deity, ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' and ''Awa-shi'' identify it as '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}'''; ''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'' and ''Dai Nihonshi Jingi-shi'' state that Yahoko deity is son or great-grandson of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}<ref>Nobukazu Nobukawa, "Suwa clan genealogy," ''Revised Suwa Genealogy, Main Volume'' (Iida Kōtarō, {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 10), p.18</ref><ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年">Hoga Toshio, ''Descendants of Nagasunehiko and Their Worshiped Shrines'', 2008</ref>. In the "Naga clan genealogy" in volume 6 of ''Shokeifu'', '''Yagami-no-Mikoto''' appears as child of Iroto-no-Mikoto (“Iroto” meaning younger sibling), and this is considered to correspond to Yahoko deity, namely {{ill|lt=Yaki-no-Mikoto|Q65249081|WD=Q65249081|1=Yachinomi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年"/>.

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okuyama Shrine
* Tenjin Shrine
* Yama Shrine
* Taishōgun Shrine

=== Cultural properties ===
; Important Cultural Properties (national)
* Wooden standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, height 108.8 cm
* Wooden standing male deity statue, height 81.2 cm
* Paper manuscript ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1 scroll) (with 8-scroll dark-blue-gold Lotus Sutra)

=== Access ===
* About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Anan Station|Q4751223|WD=Q4751223|1=Anan Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} ({{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}).

'''Yahoko Shrine''' (やほこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548|1=Nagao-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642|1=753}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}. In {{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7), it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Junior Fifth Upper. In {{ill|lt=1163|Q19672|WD=Q19672|1=1163}} ({{ill|lt=Chōkan|Q1193904|WD=Q1193904|1=Chōkan}} 1), it became an {{ill|lt=imperial-prayer temple/shrine|Q22131160|WD=Q22131160|1=Chokugansho}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nijō|Q440149|WD=Q440149|1=Emperor Nijō}}, and a dark-blue-with-gold ''{{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212|1=Lotus Sutra}}'' (later a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}<ref name="読売20220501"/>) was dedicated, along with donation of five {{ill|lt=tan (land unit)|Q11410513|WD=Q11410513|lt=tan|1=tan}} of land as {{ill|lt=temple/shrine estate|Q11458058|WD=Q11458058|lt=sacred estate|1=Jisha-ryō}}. According to ''{{ill|lt=Tosa Diary|Q844452|WD=Q844452|1=Tosa Nikki}}'', {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|Q468288|WD=Q468288|1=Ki no Tsurayuki}} prayed there to avoid {{ill|lt=pirates|Q45393|WD=Q45393|1=Piracy}}.

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} enshrining {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, related to the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}''. Its standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and standing male deity statue are designated national Important Cultural Properties. Among them, the shrine-owned paper document ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1163, Important Cultural Property<ref name="読売20220501">"Treasure of the region preserved for posterity / repository completed in Tokushima" {{ill|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|Q645218|WD=Q645218|1=The Yomiuri Shimbun}}, morning edition, May 1, 2022 (Tsumugu page)</ref>) is said to be the oldest surviving document in Tokushima Prefecture. On March 27, 2022, a completion ceremony was held for the storage building preserving these cultural properties<ref name="読売20220501"/>.

In {{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3), the shrine received an imperial visit by {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito|1=Naruhito}}. The bettō temple {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yahoko-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} is also associated.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
*:plus 34 other deities
*Regarding the deity, ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' and ''Awa-shi'' identify it as '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}'''; ''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'' and ''Dai Nihonshi Jingi-shi'' state that Yahoko deity is son or great-grandson of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}<ref>Nobukazu Nobukawa, "Suwa clan genealogy," ''Revised Suwa Genealogy, Main Volume'' (Iida Kōtarō, {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 10), p.18</ref><ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年">Hoga Toshio, ''Descendants of Nagasunehiko and Their Worshiped Shrines'', 2008</ref>. In the "Naga clan genealogy" in volume 6 of ''Shokeifu'', '''Yagami-no-Mikoto''' appears as child of Iroto-no-Mikoto (“Iroto” meaning younger sibling), and this is considered to correspond to Yahoko deity, namely {{ill|lt=Yaki-no-Mikoto|Q65249081|WD=Q65249081|1=Yachinomi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年"/>.

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okuyama Shrine
* Tenjin Shrine
* Yama Shrine
* Taishōgun Shrine

=== Cultural properties ===
; Important Cultural Properties (national)
* Wooden standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, height 108.8 cm
* Wooden standing male deity statue, height 81.2 cm
* Paper manuscript ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1 scroll) (with 8-scroll dark-blue-gold Lotus Sutra)

=== Access ===
* About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Anan Station|Q4751223|WD=Q4751223|1=Anan Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} ({{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}).

{{nihongo|'''Yahoko Shrine'''|八桙神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称= Yahoko Shrine
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=阿南市}}
|所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548|1=Nagao-chō}} Miyauchi 463
|位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
|祭神= {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
|社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
|創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642|1=753}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}
|本殿= 
|別名= Yahoko Shrine
|例祭= 
|神事=

}}
'''Yahoko Shrine''' (やほこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548|1=Nagao-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642|1=753}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}. In {{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7), it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Junior Fifth Upper. In {{ill|lt=1163|Q19672|WD=Q19672|1=1163}} ({{ill|lt=Chōkan|Q1193904|WD=Q1193904|1=Chōkan}} 1), it became an {{ill|lt=imperial-prayer temple/shrine|Q22131160|WD=Q22131160|1=Chokugansho}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nijō|Q440149|WD=Q440149|1=Emperor Nijō}}, and a dark-blue-with-gold ''{{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212|1=Lotus Sutra}}'' (later a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}<ref name="読売20220501"/>) was dedicated, along with donation of five {{ill|lt=tan (land unit)|Q11410513|WD=Q11410513|lt=tan|1=tan}} of land as {{ill|lt=temple/shrine estate|Q11458058|WD=Q11458058|lt=sacred estate|1=Jisha-ryō}}. According to ''{{ill|lt=Tosa Diary|Q844452|WD=Q844452|1=Tosa Nikki}}'', {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|Q468288|WD=Q468288|1=Ki no Tsurayuki}} prayed there to avoid {{ill|lt=pirates|Q45393|WD=Q45393|1=Piracy}}.

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} enshrining {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, related to the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}''. Its standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and standing male deity statue are designated national Important Cultural Properties. Among them, the shrine-owned paper document ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1163, Important Cultural Property<ref name="読売20220501">"Treasure of the region preserved for posterity / repository completed in Tokushima" {{ill|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|Q645218|WD=Q645218|1=The Yomiuri Shimbun}}, morning edition, May 1, 2022 (Tsumugu page)</ref>) is said to be the oldest surviving document in Tokushima Prefecture. On March 27, 2022, a completion ceremony was held for the storage building preserving these cultural properties<ref name="読売20220501"/>.

In {{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3), the shrine received an imperial visit by {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito|1=Naruhito}}. The bettō temple {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yahoko-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} is also associated.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
*:plus 34 other deities
*Regarding the deity, ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' and ''Awa-shi'' identify it as '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}'''; ''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'' and ''Dai Nihonshi Jingi-shi'' state that Yahoko deity is son or great-grandson of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}<ref>Nobukazu Nobukawa, "Suwa clan genealogy," ''Revised Suwa Genealogy, Main Volume'' (Iida Kōtarō, {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 10), p.18</ref><ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年">Hoga Toshio, ''Descendants of Nagasunehiko and Their Worshiped Shrines'', 2008</ref>. In the "Naga clan genealogy" in volume 6 of ''Shokeifu'', '''Yagami-no-Mikoto''' appears as child of Iroto-no-Mikoto (“Iroto” meaning younger sibling), and this is considered to correspond to Yahoko deity, namely {{ill|lt=Yaki-no-Mikoto|Q65249081|WD=Q65249081|1=Yachinomi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年"/>.

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okuyama Shrine
* Tenjin Shrine
* Yama Shrine
* Taishōgun Shrine

=== Cultural properties ===
; Important Cultural Properties (national)
* Wooden standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, height 108.8 cm
* Wooden standing male deity statue, height 81.2 cm
* Paper manuscript ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1 scroll) (with 8-scroll dark-blue-gold Lotus Sutra)

=== Access ===
* About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Anan Station|Q4751223|WD=Q4751223|1=Anan Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} ({{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}).

=== Notes and references ===

{{nihongo|'''Yahoko Shrine'''|八桙神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称= Yahoko Shrine
|画像= {{画像募集中|cat=阿南市}}
|所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548|1=Nagao-chō}} Miyauchi 463
|位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}

|社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}
|創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642|1=753}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}
|本殿= 
|別名= Yahoko Shrine
|例祭= 
|神事=

}}
'''Yahoko Shrine''' (やほこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in {{ill|lt=Nagao-chō|Q28684548|WD=Q28684548|1=Nagao-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}.

=== History ===
It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642|1=753}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}. In {{ill|lt=883|Q30355|WD=Q30355|1=883}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 7), it received {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of Junior Fifth Upper. In {{ill|lt=1163|Q19672|WD=Q19672|1=1163}} ({{ill|lt=Chōkan|Q1193904|WD=Q1193904|1=Chōkan}} 1), it became an {{ill|lt=imperial-prayer temple/shrine|Q22131160|WD=Q22131160|1=Chokugansho}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nijō|Q440149|WD=Q440149|1=Emperor Nijō}}, and a dark-blue-with-gold ''{{ill|lt=Lotus Sutra|Q861212|WD=Q861212|1=Lotus Sutra}}'' (later a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}<ref name="読売20220501"/>) was dedicated, along with donation of five {{ill|lt=tan (land unit)|Q11410513|WD=Q11410513|lt=tan|1=tan}} of land as {{ill|lt=temple/shrine estate|Q11458058|WD=Q11458058|lt=sacred estate|1=Jisha-ryō}}. According to ''{{ill|lt=Tosa Diary|Q844452|WD=Q844452|1=Tosa Nikki}}'', {{ill|lt=Ki no Tsurayuki|Q468288|WD=Q468288|1=Ki no Tsurayuki}} prayed there to avoid {{ill|lt=pirates|Q45393|WD=Q45393|1=Piracy}}.

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} enshrining {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}, related to the ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}''. Its standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto and standing male deity statue are designated national Important Cultural Properties. Among them, the shrine-owned paper document ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1163, Important Cultural Property<ref name="読売20220501">"Treasure of the region preserved for posterity / repository completed in Tokushima" {{ill|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|Q645218|WD=Q645218|1=The Yomiuri Shimbun}}, morning edition, May 1, 2022 (Tsumugu page)</ref>) is said to be the oldest surviving document in Tokushima Prefecture. On March 27, 2022, a completion ceremony was held for the storage building preserving these cultural properties<ref name="読売20220501"/>.

In {{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|WD=Q2062|1=1991}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 3), the shrine received an imperial visit by {{ill|lt=Naruhito|Q217096|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito|1=Naruhito}}. The bettō temple {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yahoko-ji|1=UNKNOWN}} is also associated.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}
*:plus 34 other deities
*Regarding the deity, ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' and ''Awa-shi'' identify it as '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yachihoko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}'''; ''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'' and ''Dai Nihonshi Jingi-shi'' state that Yahoko deity is son or great-grandson of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}<ref>Nobukazu Nobukawa, "Suwa clan genealogy," ''Revised Suwa Genealogy, Main Volume'' (Iida Kōtarō, {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 10), p.18</ref><ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年">Hoga Toshio, ''Descendants of Nagasunehiko and Their Worshiped Shrines'', 2008</ref>. In the "Naga clan genealogy" in volume 6 of ''Shokeifu'', '''Yagami-no-Mikoto''' appears as child of Iroto-no-Mikoto (“Iroto” meaning younger sibling), and this is considered to correspond to Yahoko deity, namely {{ill|lt=Yaki-no-Mikoto|Q65249081|WD=Q65249081|1=Yachinomi-no-Mikoto}}<ref name="宝賀寿男『長髄彦の後裔とその奉斎神社』2008年"/>.

=== Precinct shrines ===
* Okuyama Shrine
* Tenjin Shrine
* Yama Shrine
* Taishōgun Shrine

=== Cultural properties ===
; Important Cultural Properties (national)
* Wooden standing statue of Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, height 108.8 cm
* Wooden standing male deity statue, height 81.2 cm
* Paper manuscript ''Nihonke Mandokoro Kudashibumi'' (1 scroll) (with 8-scroll dark-blue-gold Lotus Sutra)

=== Access ===
* About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Anan Station|Q4751223|WD=Q4751223|1=Anan Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} ({{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}).

=== Notes and references ===

{{nihongo|'''Yahoko Shrine'''|八桙神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

|創建= (trad.) {{ill|lt=753|Q23642|WD=Q23642|1=753}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 5){{要出典|date=2023年8月}}
|本殿=
